Damage to multiple farm buildings including a roof completely blown off a barn and some walls knocked down. Wood debris scattered in multiple directions. A circular hay bail was blown a great distance. Time was estimated by radar.

Report via FB. Video and photos of a tornado with debris at the surface visible. Visual evidence of a nearby power pole snapped and strange damage to a wind break made of metal and wood. Time estimated by radar.
Concurrent with this hail report the person also reported viewing a funnel cloud to the NW of their location but no tornado.

Significant and widespread crop damage including thousands of acres of corn, peas, canola, wheat, barley and alfalfa. Photos submitted confirmed large hail mostly baseball size but up to the size of softballs, lasting for ten minutes. Multiple personal and farm vehicles and homes had their windows broken out.
Flash flooding was reported, up to a depth of 2 feet. A homeowner reported water flowing into their basement from this flash flood.
Public reported via Facebook a tornado touching down and remaining on the ground for ten minutes. A circular hay bale also caught fire from a lightning strike.
